title: 2025/10/30 Primary Production subcommittee B
displayTitle: 2025-10-30 - Primary Production Committee - Primary Production [Subcommittee B]
description: Thursday, 30 October 2025 | Primary Production Animal Welfare (Regulations for Management of Pigs) Amendment Bill
descriptionSummary: Primary Production. Animal Welfare (Regulations for Management of Pigs) Amendment Bill

Can I do a TikTok dance? No, I'm kidding. Okay, cool. So my name is Dee, and I am a vet nurse. And I was going to use this five minutes to start off with a quote and then talk a little bit about my own experience. And then if we have time at the end, just go over some questions, if anyone has any.

So the quote begins, A humane and painless end, A small price, happily paid for a life well lived, an arrangement of mutual benefit hidden by this narrative, out of sight, out of mind. Animals cease to be individuals like the pets we have at home, most known only as livestock, faceless units of production in a system of incomprehensible scale, exempt from the cruelty laws that protect our companion animals.

They're suffering unseen and unheard. They're value determined only by their usefulness to mankind rationalized in the belief of our own superiority. So that's the quote. And that is from Dominion, which is a documentary on YouTube well worth a watch and my own experience.

So I have spent my life with animals. I grew up on a lifestyle block, and of course now I'm a vet nurse, and I have a really small animal sanctuary, which I absolutely love, and I've got lots of disabled animals. It's really beautiful. And through my experience, I have grown to understand what makes us similar to animals and what makes us different.

And some of the similarities that we share is we both have the same love for our freedom. And we both have the same pain signals that go to our brain when we are being hurt. And we have the same maternal hormones when we give birth that make us love and want to care for our young.

And we're just both sentient beings that have the ability to perceive the world around us and feel happiness and sadness. And of course, we have lots of differences, both having gifts and abilities that the other doesn't. And over history, humans have used this idea of difference to label a group of individuals as less intelligent than us.

And then we've kind of used that to justify overpowering them and enslaving them to serve us. And whether we've been doing that to humans or to animals, as we have developed consciously and are now made aware of the atrocity that is occurring and has occurred repeatedly throughout history, surely we are now at a point where we are aware enough to realize that this is no longer acceptable.

Society is formed in such a way that not all people have the opportunity to be leaders. And although we are all accountable for caring for other life forms that cannot speak or help themselves, you especially have the ability to make changes that better life and create less suffering for these sentient beings.

And then I just wanted to leave with a question. So if you were a mother, or if someone that you really cared about was heavily pregnant and in a prison so small, they were unable to turn around and could only take a few steps forward and a few steps back for weeks on end, they are unable to exercise.

So their muscles weakened to the point where they can't stand up, and you develop pressure sores from the floors and lying in your own waist. And once you give birth, you watch your children being mutilated by workers with no pain relief or anesthetic. And if you were in those shoes, wouldn't you want people to stand up and make decisions that better your wellbeing?
